name: TANDOORI BEER CAN CHICKEN
servings: serves 4 or more as part of a multi-course meal
intro: This is one you are very unlikely to find at a curry house but it was too good to leave out of the book. I’ve been making beer can chicken for many years and this Indian-inspired version is one of my current favourites. You could also easily make this in a conventional oven – see my tip.

ingredients.item: x 440ml (15fl oz) can of your favourite beer or lager
Small bunch of coriander (cilantro), blended with a little water
Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

method: Blend all the marinade ingredients into a paste or pound them using a pestle and mortar.

Slowly drizzle the rapeseed (canola) oil into the paste, whisking continuously until you have a smooth emulsion. Cover the chicken inside and out with the marinade and allow to marinate for 8 hours or overnight. I carefully rub about half of the marinade under the skin but don’t worry if you can’t be bothered.
